FOREST DRIVE IN PAYSON WAS REPORTED BY THE GENERAL PUBLIC AS BEING CLOSED WITH BARRICADES WITH WATER RUNNING SWIFTLY ACROSS. NEARBY RAIN GAUGE REPORTED 1.82 INCHES OF PRECIPITATION. FROM 2:06 TO 3:37.
CAMPGROUND FLOODED NORTH OF SUPAI. CAMPSITES WASHED AWAY. CAMPERS EVACUATED. TIME ESTIMATED.

CEDAR CREEK PEAKED WITH A 5 FT RISE AND IS NOW RECEDING.

A ROCKFALL ALONG AZ SR-89 CAUSED BOTH DIRECTIONS OF THE HIGHWAY TO CLOSE AT MILEPOST 302 SOUTH OF PRESCOTT. WITHIN 30 MINUTES, TRAFFIC BEGAN ALTERNATING IN THE NORTHBOUND LANE AS CLEANUP BEGAN.
THE BIG BUG CREEK GAUGE AT SR-69 IN MAYER REPORTED A 2.9 FOOT RISE FROM 12 PM TO 1:30 PM. THE GAUGE PEAKED AT 3.9 FEET AND HAS BEGUN TO SLOWLY FALL.
WATER OVERTOPPED THE INTERSECTION OF WALKER RD. AND NEW STRIKE LN. IN WALKER. LYNX CREEK WAS HIGH BUT HAS BEGUN TO RECEDE.
HEAVY RAINS OVER THE GOODWIN FIRE SCAR BROUGHT FLASH FLOOD CONDITIONS TO BIG BUG CREEK IN CORDES LAKES AT 3:45 PM MST. VIDEO TAKEN OF THE EVENT BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC.

FLASH FLOODING CAUSED A BRIDGE TO WASHOUT LIKELY NORTH OF AZ HWY 98 ALONG INDIAN ROUTE 6330 FROM A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM THAT PASSED TO THE WEST OF THE WASHOUT LOCATION. THE PICTURES AND REPORT WERE PROVIDED BY A MEMBER OF THE ROADS DEPARTMENT OF NAVAJO NATION.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 9 SE CARRIZO. WATER WASHED ACROSS SEVERAL DRIVEWAYS BECAUSE THE CULVERTS COULDN'T HANDLE THE FLOW IN THE BAR DITCH DUE TO HEAVY RAIN IN TOWN.
SEVERAL ROADS WASHED OUT IN TOWN FROM HEAVY RAIN IN TOWN, NOT DUE TO THE RISE OF CEDAR CREEK.
FLASH FLOODING IN THE AREA ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF THE BLUE GAP STORE BADLY DAMAGED THE ROAD (HIGHWAY 4).

FLOODED URBAN ROADWAYS AND LOW-LYING AREAS IN MUCH OF EAST FLAGSTAFF. VIDEO DOCUMENTATION OF A FLASH FLOOD AT THE INTERSECTION OF BUTLER AVE AND FOURTH STREET.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 1.76 INCHES OF DAILY STORM TOTAL RAINFALL IN ASH FORK AS OF 4:36 PM MST ON JULY 15, 2018. THIS LED TO FLASH FLOODING OF DOUBLE A RANCH ROAD WITH 3-4 INCHES OF STANDING WATER ON THE ROADWAYS. PORTIONS OF DIRT ROADWAYS WERE WASHED AWAY AS A RESULT FROM THE LOCALIZED FLOODING.

BIG BUG CREEK EXPERIENCED FLASH FLOOD CONDITIONS DURING MID-AFTERNOON WHICH LASTED FOR APPROXIMATELY TWO HOURS, AS REPORTED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER. WATER ROSE OUT OF THE BANKS AND FLOODED E ANTELOPE RD DURING THAT TIME FRAME. WINDS ALSO GUSTED UP TO 45 MPH AS A STORM PASSED BETWEEN 3:15 - 4:15 PM MST.
FLOODING IN DIAMOND VALLEY AREA. DIAMOND DRIVE WASHED OUT, AND SIGNIFICANT FLOODING OVER ROADS IN THE NEIGHBORHOOD. TIME ESTIMATED.

LYNX CREEK IMMEDIATELY UPSTREAM OF FAIN LAKE (IN FAIN PARK) WAS EXPERIENCING FLASH FLOOD CONDITIONS THAT WERE CAUSING THE CREEK WATER TO FLOW OUT OF THE BANKS. THE FLOODING CONTINUED, AS FAR UPSTREAM AS COSTCO, AS LYNX CREEK RUNS PARALLEL TO US HWY 169.
MEASURED 1.75 RAINFALL IN ONE HOUR. WATER OVER LOCAL ROADS, AND SOME FLOOD DAMAGE TO PRIVATE ROADS.
GLASSFORD HILL RD CLOSED NEXT TO BRADSHAW HIGH SCHOOL DUE TO MUD FROM NEW SUBDIVISION. BRADSHAW HIGH SCHOOL HAD WATER IN THE GYM LIKELY FROM AREA FLOODING.
